reasoning: Initial observation shows d1=10, d2=20, d3=30 and total_distance=60. Summing these gives 60, matching total_distance. To verify this hypothesis, I will increase d1 by 1 unit and observe if total_distance increases by 1 unit.

The initial observation (d1=10, d2=20, d3=30, total=60) and the result after increasing d1 to 20 (total=70) strongly suggest that total_distance is the sum of d1, d2, and d3. The difference in total_distance (70-60=10) matches the difference in d1 (20-10=10). To further verify this linear relationship, I will increase d2 by 1 unit and check if total_distance increases by exactly 1 unit.

Based on the history, increasing d1 by 10 increased total_distance by 10, and increasing d2 by 10 increased total_distance by 10. This confirms a linear additive relationship. To be thorough and confirm the coefficient for d3 is also 1, I will increase d3 by 1 unit and verify the total_distance increases by exactly 1 unit.

The history shows that increasing d1 by 10 increased total_distance by 10, increasing d2 by 10 increased total_distance by 10, and increasing d3 by 10 increased total_distance by 10. This consistently demonstrates that total_distance is the sum of d1, d2, and d3. The current state (d1=20, d2=30, d3=40) sums to 90, which matches the observed total_distance. I have sufficient evidence to conclude the equation.
